message:
🐛 fix: persist JWT secret to ~/.kubestellar/ across curl reinstalls (#8467)

When users reinstall via `curl | bash`, the binary lands in a fresh
directory with a new random JWT secret. Existing browser sessions
signed with the old secret fail with "token signature is invalid",
showing a confusing `token_exchange_failed` on the login page.

Fix: loadOrCreateDevSecret() now checks ~/.kubestellar/.jwt-secret
as a shared fallback before generating a new secret. The local
.jwt-secret still takes priority (explicit override). New secrets
are written to both locations so reinstalls reuse the same key and
existing browser sessions remain valid.

Fixes #8202

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
